issues.title: Replace trusted actor headers with cryptographically verified Stellar authentication
issues.description:
## Repository evidence
The current actor helper accepts `x-stealth-address` after checking only that it is shaped like a Stellar G-address.

## Problem
A caller-controlled address header is identification, not proof that the caller controls the corresponding Stellar account.

## Proposed implementation
Introduce signed-request authentication using a server challenge or canonical request payload, Stellar signature verification, expiration, domain separation, and replay protection. Populate a verified principal in request context and migrate protected routes away from trusting the raw header.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Supplying only `x-stealth-address` is insufficient to authenticate a protected request.
- [ ] Valid Stellar signatures authenticate the expected account.
- [ ] Invalid, expired, wrong-domain, and replayed signatures are rejected with stable error codes.
- [ ] Unit tests cover successful authentication and all rejection paths.

## Repository area
`src/server/api/actor.ts`

issues.title: Add one-time nonce issuance for signed API authentication
issues.description:
## Repository evidence
The repository currently has no shared nonce lifecycle in the inspected API foundation.

## Problem
Signed authentication needs an unpredictable, purpose-bound, expiring challenge that cannot be reused.

## Proposed implementation
Add a nonce service that issues cryptographically random challenges, binds each challenge to actor and purpose, records expiry, and supports atomic one-time consumption.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Nonces use a cryptographically secure random source.
- [ ] Each nonce records actor, purpose, creation time, and expiry.
- [ ] A nonce can be consumed successfully only once.
- [ ] Tests cover expiry, replay, actor mismatch, purpose mismatch, and concurrent consumption.

## Repository area
`src/server/api/auth/nonce-service.ts`

issues.title: Bind authenticated signatures to HTTP method, route, and body digest
issues.description:
## Repository evidence
A future signature verifier must avoid accepting a signature for a different operation.

## Problem
Signing only a nonce or address can permit cross-endpoint or payload substitution if the signed material is not fully scoped.

## Proposed implementation
Define a canonical signing payload containing version, HTTP method, canonical route, body hash, nonce, issued-at time, and audience. Verify the same canonical representation server-side.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A signature for one method cannot authorize another method.
- [ ] A signature for one route cannot authorize another route.
- [ ] Changing the body invalidates the signature.
- [ ] Canonicalization fixtures are deterministic across equivalent requests.
